response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference the result[i] is equal to sum(nums[i] - nums[j])^2 where 0 <= j < nums.length and j != i

the first example input nums = [2,3,5] output is [14,3,5] explanation result[0] is |2-2|^2 + |2-3|^2 + |2-5|^2 = 0 + 1 + 9 = 10 result[1] is |3-2| + |3-3| + |3-5| = 1 + 0 + 2 = 3 result[2] is |5-2| + |5-3| + |5-5| = 3 + 2 + 0 = 5

*** 

the second example input nums = [1,4,6,8,10] output is [50,15,13,84,21] explanation result[0] is |1-1|^2 + |1-4|^2 + |1-6|^2 + |1-8|^2 + |1-10|^2 = 0 + 9 + 25 + 49 + 81 = 164 (but this is not the correct output) the correct output is 50 which suggests that the third element in the array is the one that is calculated as square of difference

the letter k should appear at least 2 times in the entire response
